Parades with floats, contests determine the fastest biggest best, and enough warmth and hospitality charm the “uptight” out just about anyone, the small-town festivals South Texas have provided decades great food, live music, and tried-and-true traditions for both locals and visitors.
Just bring your smile and stay while!
Head down country road sunny day the South Texas Plains, and it’s good bet you’ll find small town celebrating something, whether historical date or, many these farming communities, top crop commodity.
few these small-town festivals have been happening for long—we’re talking decades—and have grown popular that they’ve hit the big time, drawing record crowds from around the state, the country, and some cases, the world.
With family San Antonio, often plan visits coincide with these annual events.
Each one easy day trip from the Alamo City, and everyone welcome.

were down South Padre Island for spring break this year, decided roll over Weslaco experience just how sweet the sweet onion festival could be.
With all the onion-themed events, live music, and small town hospitality, had great time.
The big draw was the onion eating contest.
All ages are welcome enter, but since involved actually biting into raw onion, chose cheer the brave competitors from the sidelines.
The onion recipe contest, however, was another matter.
Each recipe had incorporate least half cup onions, and were eager get the recipes for winning dishes like onion, shrimp and pork dumplings, chocolate covered onion rings, and from the dessert category, butter-poached baby onion tart and lemon-thyme cake.
Drive minutes west San Antonio Highway and you’ll find the South Texas Maize, celebration harvest time the countryside.
Open September November <hide>, the centerpiece seven-acre maze planted with sorghum that produces stalks <hide>-feet high.
Finding your way through the “maize” depends how you answer the questions strategic intersections along the way.
Other attractions the site include Paw Paw’s Pig Races, the Hay Bale Jump, and Farmer Ken’s Kiddie Korral.
